Tests string and number concatenation in JavaScript using an assert statement.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the behavior of JavaScript's type coercion and string concatenation. It evaluates whether the expression `"1" + 1 + 1` is equal to the expression `("1" + 1) + 1`. The test demonstrates how JavaScript concatenates strings and numbers, highlighting that the first expression results in the string `"111"`, while the second expression also results in `"111"`, confirming that both expressions are equivalent. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript's type conversion and operator precedence.
